claim: Bolt.diy (formerly oTToDev) is now the official open-source version of Bolt.new, partnered with StackBlitz, enabling anyone to run and modify a full-stack AI coding assistant
actionable: Use Bolt.diy as a self-hosted alternative to proprietary Bolt.new for building web applications with AI agents
source_title: BREAKING NEWS: oTToDev is Now the OFFICIAL Open Source Bolt.new

claim: Frontier AI research labs (training GPT-5, Claude, etc.) use Bolt.diy as a benchmark to evaluate new model capabilities because it represents real-world full-stack application building
actionable: Reference Bolt.diy or similar full-stack benchmarks when evaluating new model releases for production readiness
source_title: BREAKING NEWS: oTToDev is Now the OFFICIAL Open Source Bolt.new

claim: Bolt.diy enables testing any LLM (local or proprietary) with a single swap, making it easy to compare model quality across different providers on real-world coding tasks
actionable: Use Bolt.diy to quickly evaluate new coding models by swapping them in-flight without rebuilding the interface
source_title: BREAKING NEWS: oTToDev is Now the OFFICIAL Open Source Bolt.new

claim: Educational use of Bolt.diy helps developers learn how to effectively collaborate with AI coding agents, filling a gap since most developers are unfamiliar with agentic code generation workflows
actionable: Use Bolt.diy as an educational platform to practice agentic coding workflows and learn best practices for human-AI collaboration
source_title: BREAKING NEWS: oTToDev is Now the OFFICIAL Open Source Bolt.new
